subject: introverts
one_liner: Video calls for people who would rather not be on the video call.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan enterprise calm. Treats social withdrawal as a premium, fully-instrumented workplace feature. Sells absence as productivity. Never winks, never apologizes, never admits the product is sad.

hero.sub: Lurk keeps your camera off, your status green, and a calm AI version of you nodding along while you go do literally anything else. Attendance, without the attending.

features.title: Stand-In Mode
features.blurb: We render a composed, attentive version of you and loop it for up to four hours. It holds eye contact with whoever's talking. It tilts its head when someone says 'to your point.' It never asks a follow-up question. You are free to make tea.

features.title: Canned Sincerity
features.blurb: Tap once for 'great point.' Tap twice for 'building on that.' Every phrase was workshopped by a real linguist to sound like you meant it. You did not mean it. Nobody can tell.

features.title: The Quiet Exit
features.blurb: Leave whenever you want. Lurk holds your tile, keeps your dot green, and tells anyone who @-mentions you that you're 'just pulling up the deck.' There is no deck. There was never a deck.

pricing_tiers.name: Legally Present
pricing_tiers.tagline: For people who are paid to attend.
perks: AI avatar with three board-approved facial expressions
Entire meetings attended on your behalf, gavel to gavel

testimonials.quote: I haven't spoken in a standup since March. My skip-level says I've never been more aligned.
testimonials.name: Dwight Pell
testimonials.title: Staff Engineer, perpetually 'half-listening'

testimonials.quote: The Stand-In nodded through my own performance review. I got a Meets Expectations.
testimonials.name: Carol Nuñez
testimonials.title: Senior Program Manager, two cameras off

testimonials.quote: I attended four all-hands at the same time. Lurk rendered me in all of them, calmly.
testimonials.name: Theo Brandt-Sorensen
testimonials.title: Director of Synchronous Operations (fully remote)

demo_screen_concept: A live meeting-room UI. A grid of six participant tiles; your own tile shows your AI Stand-In with a dim amber 'engaged' ring and a small AI badge. A right rail holds one-tap Canned Sincerity reactions, a Presence Score gauge reading 87, a 'Stand-In: looping' toggle, and a red 'Leave quietly' button that plays no leave sound. Tapping a canned reaction floats it into the chat log and nudges your Presence Score up. Toggling Stand-In swaps your tile between a frozen frame and the looping avatar. Muting yourself dims the whole room a shade, as a reward.

design_direction.style: Dimmed do-not-disturb interface. A deliberately low-stimulation warm-dark UI built to recede rather than demand attention. Soft warm-charcoal ground, hushed warm-white type kept at comfortable contrast, hairline warm-gray rules, and a single dimmed-amber indicator lamp as the only bright object on the page (primary CTA, the live 'engaged' dot, the presence pulse). A muted sage marks calm and mute states. Generous negative space, no decorative gradients, no glow, no glassy blur, no rounded blobs. Large quiet Newsreader headings, optical-sized and low-contrast, against a Spline Sans body, with DM Mono for every number, score, and status label. Reads like a calm-tech focus product from a company that considers being perceived a bug.
design_direction.archetype: Calm-tech / focus-app product landing rendered as a dimmed do-not-disturb panel: a hushed left-aligned hero with one amber lamp, three feature rows, three pull-quote testimonials, a three-tier pricing rack, and a demo handoff to the meeting room.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Slow and minimal. A soft amber lamp pulse on a long loop, a 1px hover lift, 180-240ms ease-out fades. No bounce, no slide-in carnival, no parallax. Things appear quietly and respect prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Left-aligned hero: an oversized quiet headline, one muted sub line, an amber primary CTA beside a ghost 'open the demo' link. To the right, a small mock meeting tile showing the AI Stand-In with a dim amber engaged ring and a green status dot. Vast warm-charcoal negative space. A thin top bar carries the wordmark on the left and a single 'status: away' chip on the right.
